items.title: Waste burning trial at Adbri cement site prompts backlash from council

items.title: Brett McMaster avoids jail for trafficking cocaine while Australian Army soldier
items.summary: The former rifleman and field medic sourced cocaine from a fellow Townsville soldier and on-sold the drugs to army colleagues.

items.title: Man found guilty of murdering two-year-old boy at Coffs Harbour
items.summary: A jury heard the boy was rushed to hospital after he was found with a catastrophic brain injury and a fractured pelvis and rib.

items.title: Tribunal upholds three-match ban for Crows forward Darcy Fogarty
items.summary: The Crows fail in their challenge to Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as the panel finds Fogarty had time to decide to swing his arm at Sydney's Harry Cunningham. Follow live.

items.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader
items.summary: South Australia's largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state's most likely to fall to One Nation.

items.title: Mount Barker restaurant owners Wok the Duck launch petition against government's land acquisition
items.summary: The owners of a Mount Barker restaurant have launched a petition to block the government's proposed acquisition of the site on which their business stands.

items.title: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors
items.summary: A federal law that threatens doctors if they use electronic communications, such as the internet or phone, to discuss voluntary assisted dying remains in force, even though telehealth is now an established part of healthcare.

items.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah sues Bendigo council, La Trobe University over Bendigo Writers Festival
items.summary: Randa Abdel-Fattah launches legal action after Bendigo Writers Festival organisers sent a code of conduct that sparked censorship concerns and led to the boycott of dozens of authors.

items.title: Imalu Tigers, Tapalinga Super Stars crowned premiers at 2026 Tiwi Islands football grand finals

items.title: 'ISIS bride' Rayann El Houli granted bail by Melbourne court

items.title: Australia's Bernard Tomic wins first Challenger Tour title in eight years

items.title: Four easy one-pot dinners that are perfect for winter

items.title: North Queensland school stabbing accused released on strict bail, victim discharged from hospital

items.title: Woman in serious condition as police investigate stabbing in Kalgoorlie

items.title: Paramedics told to take complex cardiac patients to Launceston General Hospital instead of Mersey

items.summary: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

items.summary: Australia this week made an important step forward in grappling with the AI revolution. Meanwhile, in Melbourne a company is making humanoid robots.

items.summary: Pauline Hanson's grievance politics is a challenge for Labor, but a splitting headache for the Liberals — compounded by former prime minister Tony Abbott.
